name: Instagram Profile Picture
description: Downloads an image from a URL, then sets it as the Instagram profile picture via Edit Profile.

params.imageUrl: Direct URL to the image file to use as profile picture

steps.goal: Download an image file to the device. Use the download action with url {{imageUrl}} and save it as {{albumName}}/profile_pic.jpg. After the download completes, mark done.

Open Instagram. If you see any popup or dialog (like 'Not now', 'Skip', account suggestions, or a Google sign-in prompt), dismiss it by tapping 'Not now', 'Skip', 'X', or pressing Back. If you see someone else's profile or a post, press Back until you see the home feed. To navigate to your own profile, look for a small profile picture or avatar icon in the bottom-right corner of the screen and tap it. If you can't find the profile icon in the element list, tap at the bottom-right area of the screen (coordinates approximately [990, 2170] on a 1080-wide screen). Once on your profile page (you should see 'Edit profile' button, your posts count, followers count), tap 'Edit profile'. Wait for the Edit Profile screen to load — you should see fields like Name, Username, Bio, and your profile picture.

You are on the Edit Profile screen. Tap on the profile picture (the circular avatar image near the top, or the 'Edit picture' / 'Change photo' text). A menu should appear with options like 'Choose from library', 'Take photo', etc. Tap 'Choose from library' (or 'New profile picture' then 'Choose from library'). If a permissions dialog appears, tap 'Allow'.

A photo picker or gallery is now open. You need to find the image from the '{{albumName}}' album. Look for a dropdown at the top (e.g. 'Recents') and tap it. Then look for 'All albums' or 'Other...' and tap it. Find and tap the album named '{{albumName}}'. Tap the image to select it. Then tap 'Done', 'Next', or the checkmark to confirm the selection. If Instagram shows a crop/resize screen, tap 'Done' or the checkmark. Wait for the profile picture to update, then mark done.
